perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be in full force and effect commencing on January 2, 2020 and shall remain in effect for one (1) year or until Consultant completes the services requested. Either Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement without notice in the event of the bankruptcy, insolvency, or assignment for the benefit of creditors of the other Party. Either Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement with notice, and the effective date of termination shall be the date such notice is received (by mail, overnight delivery, or fax) by the terminated Party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be in full force and effect commencing on January 2, 2020 and shall remain in effect for one (1) year, regardless of whether Consultant completes the services requested. Either Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement without notice in the event of the bankruptcy, insolvency, or assignment for the benefit of creditors of the other Party. This Agreement can only be terminated by mutual written consent of both parties, and no other form of termination is permitted.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for termination with notice by either party. The changed text contradicts this by stating that the agreement can *only* be terminated by mutual written consent, and the term will last for one year, regardless if the consultant completes the services or not, meaning even if the services are completed before the year ends, the contract is still active. This contradicts the earlier provision allowing termination with notice and creates uncertainty about the conditions under which the agreement can be terminated. This is an in-text contradiction because now there are two scenarios in the contract on how the contract can be terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be in full force and effect commencing on January 2, 2020 and shall remain in effect for one (1) year, regardless of whether Consultant completes the services requested. Either Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement without notice in the event of the bankruptcy, insolvency, or assignment for the benefit of creditors of the other Party. This Agreement can only be terminated by mutual written consent of both parties, and no other form of termination is permitted.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by stating that 'This Agreement can only be terminated by mutual written consent of both parties, and no other form of termination is permitted.' This directly conflicts with the earlier clause within the same section (which is also the contradicted text) that allows either party to terminate the agreement under specific circumstances (bankruptcy, insolvency, etc.) or with notice, creating an in-text contradiction, by providing contradictory and irreconcilable methods of termination.

perturbation.original_text: As Consultant's entire compensation for its performance under this agreement, the Company shall pay Consultant $250,000,00 cash compensation. In addition, the Consultant shall earn additional cash compensation for the sale of any inventory/assets that were acquired in the acquisition of TCBM Holdings, LLC on November 30, 2019. Any cash compensation paid to Consultant shall be treated as a reduction in principal to the Convertible Note ("Note") issued by the Company to Jetco Holdings, LLC ("Jetco") on November 30, 2019. For example: The $250,000 cash compensation paid to Consultant shall reduce the Jetco Note principal amount by $250,000. In the event the Consultant were to sale $100,000 of inventory held by the Company, the payout of $100,000 to Consultant will reduce the principal on the Jetco Note by $100,000. Additional compensation to the Consultant for future products sold by the Company, or revenue earned by the Company for services provided will be negotiated by the Company and Consultant at the time of potential revenue recognition. The Consultant will be solely responsible for all tax returns and payments required to be filed with or made to any federal, state or local tax authority with respect to the Consultant's performance of services and receipt of fees under this Agreement. The Company will regularly report amounts paid, if any, to the Consultant by filing Form 1099-MISC and/or other appropriate form with the Internal Revenue Service as required by law.
perturbation.changed_text: As Consultant's entire compensation for its performance under this agreement, the Company shall pay Consultant $250,000.00 cash compensation. This compensation will be paid in monthly installments of $20,833.33, starting January 2, 2020. In addition, the Consultant *may* earn additional cash compensation for the sale of any inventory/assets that were acquired in the acquisition of TCBM Holdings, LLC on November 30, 2019, if the Board of Directors deems it appropriate. Any cash compensation paid to Consultant shall be treated as a reduction in principal to the Convertible Note ("Note") issued by the Company to Jetco Holdings, LLC ("Jetco") on November 30, 2019. For example: The $250,000 cash compensation paid to Consultant shall reduce the Jetco Note principal amount by $250,000. In the event the Consultant were to sale $100,000 of inventory held by the Company, the payout of $100,000 to Consultant will reduce the principal on the Jetco Note by $100,000. Additional compensation to the Consultant for future products sold by the Company, or revenue earned by the Company for services provided will be negotiated by the Company and Consultant at the time of potential revenue recognition. The Consultant will be solely responsible for all tax returns and payments required to be filed with or made to any federal, state or local tax authority with respect to the Consultant's performance of services and receipt of fees under this Agreement. The Company will regularly report amounts paid, if any, to the Consultant by filing Form 1099-MISC and/or other appropriate form with the Internal Revenue Service as required by law.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the consultant *shall* earn additional cash compensation for sales. The modified text introduces uncertainty by stating that the consultant *may* earn additional compensation and it depends on the Board of Directors, implying that this is not guaranteed and at their discresion. This contradicts the original definite statement. This impacts the enforceability of the compensation terms. This is an in-text contradiction because it changes a 'shall' to a 'may'. Also, the term regarding the $250,000.00 compensation is being paid out. The original text never stated that the money was going to be paid out, instead was just treating as a reduction in principal to the Convertible Note.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As Consultant's entire compensation for its performance under this agreement, the Company shall pay Consultant $250,000.00 cash compensation. This compensation will be paid in monthly installments of $20,833.33, starting January 2, 2020. In addition, the Consultant *may* earn additional cash compensation for the sale of any inventory/assets that were acquired in the acquisition of TCBM Holdings, LLC on November 30, 2019, if the Board of Directors deems it appropriate. Any cash compensation paid to Consultant shall be treated as a reduction in principal to the Convertible Note ("Note") issued by the Company to Jetco Holdings, LLC ("Jetco") on November 30, 2019. For example: The $250,000 cash compensation paid to Consultant shall reduce the Jetco Note principal amount by $250,000. In the event the Consultant were to sale $100,000 of inventory held by the Company, the payout of $100,000 to Consultant will reduce the principal on the Jetco Note by $100,000. Additional compensation to the Consultant for future products sold by the Company, or revenue earned by the Company for services provided will be negotiated by the Company and Consultant at the time of potential revenue recognition. The Consultant will be solely responsible for all tax returns and payments required to be filed with or made to any federal, state or local tax authority with respect to the Consultant's performance of services and receipt of fees under this Agreement. The Company will regularly report amounts paid, if any, to the Consultant by filing Form 1099-MISC and/or other appropriate form with the Internal Revenue Service as required by law.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction regarding the additional compensation for the sale of inventory/assets. The original text implies that the consultant 'shall' earn additional compensation. However, the changed text uses 'may' and adds the condition that the Board of Directors deems it appropriate, making the compensation discretionary rather than guaranteed. This directly contradicts the original implied obligation and introduces uncertainty, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Company further acknowledges and agrees that the identity of the subject Opportunities, and all other information concerning an Opportunity (including without limitation, all mailing information, phone and fax numbers, email addresses and other contact information) introduced hereunder are the property of Consultant, and shall be treated as confidential information by the Company, it affiliates, officers, directors, shareholders, employees, agents, representatives, successors and assigns. The Company shall not use such information, except in the context of any arrangement with Consultant in which Consultant is directly and actively involved, and never without Consultant's prior written approval. The Company further agrees that neither it nor its employees, affiliates or assigns, shall enter into, or otherwise arrange (either for it/him/herself, or any other person or entity) any business relationship, contact any person regarding such Opportunity, either directly or indirectly, or any of its affiliates, or accept any compensation or advantage in relation to such Opportunity except as directly though Consultant, without the prior written approval of Consultant.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company further acknowledges and agrees that the identity of the subject Opportunities, and all other information concerning an Opportunity (including without limitation, all mailing information, phone and fax numbers, email addresses and other contact information) introduced hereunder are the property of Consultant, and shall be treated as confidential information by the Company, it affiliates, officers, directors, shareholders, employees, agents, representatives, successors and assigns. The Company shall not use such information, except in the context of any arrangement with Consultant in which Consultant is directly and actively involved, and never without Consultant's prior written approval. The Company further agrees that neither it nor its employees, affiliates or assigns, *may*, without violating the agreement, enter into, or otherwise arrange (either for it/him/herself, or any other person or entity) any business relationship, contact any person regarding such Opportunity, either directly or indirectly, or any of its affiliates, or accept any compensation or advantage in relation to such Opportunity except as directly though Consultant, without the prior written approval of Consultant.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that the company 'shall not' enter into any business relationship related to the opportunity without the consultant's approval. By contrast, the modified text uses 'may' enter into the relationship without violating the agreement if they have the consultant's approval. This creates ambiguity around the circumstances where the company can take independent action with the consultant's approval. This undermines the non-circumvention clause. This in-text contradiction exists because instead of saying the company 'shall not', it 'may' enter into the business relationship without the consent of the consultant, effectively allowing for an exception that was not stated in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company further acknowledges and agrees that the identity of the subject Opportunities, and all other information concerning an Opportunity (including without limitation, all mailing information, phone and fax numbers, email addresses and other contact information) introduced hereunder are the property of Consultant, and shall be treated as confidential information by the Company, it affiliates, officers, directors, shareholders, employees, agents, representatives, successors and assigns. The Company shall not use such information, except in the context of any arrangement with Consultant in which Consultant is directly and actively involved, and never without Consultant's prior written approval. The Company further agrees that neither it nor its employees, affiliates or assigns, *may*, without violating the agreement, enter into, or otherwise arrange (either for it/him/herself, or any other person or entity) any business relationship, contact any person regarding such Opportunity, either directly or indirectly, or any of its affiliates, or accept any compensation or advantage in relation to such Opportunity except as directly though Consultant, without the prior written approval of Consultant.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits the company from engaging in any business relationship related to the opportunity without the consultant's approval ('shall not'). The changed text introduces 'may', suggesting the company might engage in such relationships with approval, thereby introducing ambiguity where the original text was absolute. This contradicts the expectation of a strict non-circumvention agreement.
